questions.prompt: Your agent forgot the user's order ID from turn 3 by turn 20. The user had to repeat it. Best architectural fix?

choices.text: Add structured task state alongside the chat history. Critical facts (order ID, user email, decisions made) live in a small structured object that gets passed into the system prompt each turn. The chat history can be sliding-window-compacted; the structured state persists in full.

questions.explanation: Structured task state separates 'critical facts that must persist' from 'chat texture that can be summarized.' The agent sees the structured state on every turn; the chat history is bounded by compaction. Both can coexist — and should.

questions.prompt: What's the production short-term memory pattern that scales to long conversations?

choices.text: Sliding window of recent turns verbatim + rolling summary of older turns + structured task state for critical facts. Stack with prompt caching for further cost reduction. Per-turn input stays bounded; agent doesn't forget the key facts.

questions.explanation: The production pattern is three layers: verbatim recent (immediate continuity), summarized older (gist preserved), structured state (critical facts in full). Combined with caching, per-turn cost stays roughly constant even across 100+ turn conversations.
